# Architecture 2.0 Flowchart {#sec-appendix-d-flowchart}

```{=latex}
\abstract*{This appendix provides a visual flowchart and cheat sheet summarizing the Architecture 2.0 loop, tracing the process from the initial study brief to the final sign-off by the human architect.}
```

The Architecture 2.0 loop relies on a strict separation between work delegated to models, tools, or scripts and responsibility assigned to the human architect. The flowchart below illustrates how the eight distinct roles (Generation, Prediction, Optimization, Critique, Repair, Verification, Explanation, and Coordination) interact throughout the design process to culminate in the architect's final sign-off.

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-arch-loop-flowchart
%%| fig-cap: "The Architecture 2.0 Loop. Models and tools execute candidates, but the human architect owns the intent, evidence boundary, and final decision."

graph TD
    %% Define Styles
    classDef human fill:#d4e6f1,stroke:#2980b9,stroke-width:2px,color:#000
    classDef aiRole fill:#e8daef,stroke:#8e44ad,stroke-width:2px,color:#000
    classDef tool fill:#d5f5e3,stroke:#27ae60,stroke-width:2px,color:#000
    classDef review fill:#fcf3cf,stroke:#f1c40f,stroke-width:2px,color:#000

    %% Nodes
    A["Human Architect<br>(Defines Intent & Scope)"]:::human
    B["Coordination<br>(Routes Tasks)"]:::aiRole
    C["Generation<br>(Proposes Candidates)"]:::aiRole
    D["Prediction & Simulation<br>(Evaluates Hardware Proxies)"]:::tool
    E{"Meets Objectives?"}:::review
    F["Critique & Repair<br>(Diagnoses Failures)"]:::aiRole
    G["Optimization<br>(Refines Parameters)"]:::aiRole
    H["Verification & Explanation<br>(Checks Bounds & Explains)"]:::tool
    I["Evidence Review<br>(Card & Signoff)"]:::review
    J["Accountable Decision<br>(Human Final Sign-Off)"]:::human

    %% Flow
    A -->|1. Prompts & Study Brief| B
    B -->|2. Delegates Work| C
    C -->|3. Submits Candidate| D
    D -->|4. Proxy Result| E
    E -- "No" --> F
    F -->|5. Revised Candidate| D
    E -- "Yes" --> G
    G -->|6. Tuned Candidate| H
    H -->|7. Formal Checks & Logs| I
    I -->|8. Presents Evidence| J
    A -.->|Retains Final Authority| J
```

## The Eight AI Roles in Practice

In practice, these eight AI roles function interdependently to execute the design loop. Coordination models first interpret the architect's study brief and allocate specific tasks across the system. Generation models then construct design candidates, system configurations, and Verilog blocks. Once a candidate exists, Prediction models estimate performance and power metrics using surrogate representations. Optimization routines refine these candidates through extensive parameter sweeps and design-space exploration. Throughout this process, Critique models actively identify violations in design rules or constraints, allowing Repair models to dynamically generate fixes whenever simulations fail. Before presenting any results to the architect, Verification models formally validate memory coherence, functional correctness, and physical layout rules. Finally, Explanation models summarize the resulting simulation telemetry and trace causality to provide a legible evidence package for human review.

This explicit structure ensures the design process never treats the mere generation of a candidate as the validation of an architecture. Ground truth is established solely through the rigorous simulation and verification flow, leaving the final design decision strictly in the hands of the accountable human architect.
